pattern: An exact cover holds only when the small rectangles' areas sum to the bounding box and every interior corner cancels in pairs, leaving exactly the four outer corners unpaired.
hints: If the pieces tile a single rectangle with no gaps or overlaps, their combined area must equal the area of the smallest box that contains them all.
Track every corner point of every small rectangle; a point shared by an even number of rectangles is an interior junction, while an odd count marks an exposed corner.
After processing all pieces, only the four corners of the overall bounding box may remain with an odd (exactly one) appearance — all other points must cancel — and the area test must also pass.

edge_inputs.description: five pieces forming a perfect square
a gap in the middle leaves the cover incomplete
overlapping pieces inflate the area so the corner test alone is not enough
a single rectangle is trivially perfect
two pieces that share an edge but together are an L, not a rectangle
negative coordinates with a clean 2x1 split
correct total area but pieces overlap so corners do not cancel

stress.description: tile a wide grid into unit squares so the cover is always perfect
stress.note: always a valid full tiling; tests the area+corner accounting at scale

stress.description: tile a grid but randomly drop one cell so most cases are imperfect
stress.note: dropping an interior or edge cell breaks the cover; some runs stay perfect
